engineIntelligence.h2: MG MGB Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the MG MGB in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for classic car restorations.

cards.altText: MG MGB 18V engine - petrol 1.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Rear main seal oil leaks - common after 60k miles, causes oil pooling under engine bay.
Cooling system overheating - aged radiators and water pumps fail, leading to head gasket stress.
Bearing wear at high mileage - main and big-end bearings degrade, causing knocking noises.

cards.altText: MG MGB 18G engine - petrol 1.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Rocker cover gasket leaks - top-end oil seepage common on early B-Series units.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise at idle, requires preventative replacement.
Cylinder bore wear - low compression on high-mileage examples without rebuild history.

cards.altText: MG MGB 35S4 engine - petrol 3.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Aluminium block cooling issues - overheating if radiator or water pump not upgraded.
Fuel system component wear - SU carburettors require specialist calibration for reliability.
Oil seal degradation - V8-specific seals fail earlier than B-Series equivalents.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £4,200-£8,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Rover 3.5L units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: MG MGB Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the MG MGB across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Early B-Series powerplant (1962-1967), defined the MGB's character with simple OHV design and reliable torque delivery.
commonFailures: Rocker cover gasket oil leaks - top-end seepage common after 50k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling at idle, preventative replacement recommended
Cylinder bore wear - low compression on high-mileage units without rebuild history
entries.cta: Get quotes for 18G MG MGB engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Refined late-production B-Series (1968-1980), most common MGB engine with updated cooling and gasket specifications.
compatibleVariants: Roadster (1968-1980), GT (1968-1980), Limited Edition (1979), Rubber Bumper models
commonFailures: Rear main seal oil leaks - common after 60k miles, causes oil pooling under engine
Cooling system overheating - aged radiators and water pumps fail, head gasket stress
Bearing wear at high mileage - main and big-end bearings degrade, causing knocking
entries.cta: Get quotes for 18V MG MGB engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Emissions-compliant late B-Series (1975-1980), calibrated for US federal standards with updated carburettor jets.
compatibleVariants: Rubber Bumper Roadster, Rubber Bumper GT, US Specification models, Limited Edition
commonFailures: Carburettor jet clogging - emissions calibration sensitive to fuel quality variations
Exhaust manifold gasket leaks - heat cycling causes seal degradation on late models
Valve guide wear - oil consumption increases at idle on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 18V-Z MG MGB engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Lightweight aluminium Rover V8 fitted to GT V8 (1973-1976), delivered 137hp with distinctive torque character for classic performance.
commonFailures: Aluminium block cooling issues - overheating if radiator or water pump not upgraded
Fuel system component wear - SU carburettors require specialist calibration for reliability
Oil seal degradation - V8-specific seals fail earlier than B-Series equivalents
entries.cta: Get quotes for 35S4 MG MGB engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production Rover V8 variant (1975-1976) with updated fuel system components for improved reliability in GT V8 applications.
commonFailures: Fuel pump diaphragm wear - late-model V8s experience pump failure after 70k miles
Exhaust manifold cracking - heat cycling causes stress fractures on aluminium components
Timing cover oil leaks - front seal degradation common on high-mileage V8 units

commonProblems.h2: MG MGB Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The MG MGB's B-Series engines are robust but age-related failures are common - oil leaks from rear main seals, overheating from aged cooling systems, and bearing wear at high mileage dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Rear Main Seal Oil Leaks - Why MG MGB B-Series Engines Drip Oil
problems.rootCause: Original cork/rubber rear main seal design degrades with age and heat cycling, allowing oil to escape between engine block and gearbox.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Seal Replacement Only - Remove gearbox, replace rear main seal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Gearbox removal, seal replacement, reassembly, oil refill
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; other aged seals may fail soon after

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Seal Kit - Replace all major oil seals during rebuild prep
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip to short block, replace rear main, rocker, sump seals
repairOptions.longevity: Better value if planning partial rebuild; addresses multiple leak points

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete unit with upgraded seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B-Series with upgraded rear main seal design, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; eliminates leak risk with warranty coverage

problems.recommendation: On a classic MGB worth £8,000-£25,000, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded seals offers better value than repeated seal replacements. If the engine is otherwise healthy and mileage is under 60k, a specialist seal replacement may suffice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B-Series engine prices for your MG MGB

problems.h4: Cooling System Overheating - Why MG MGB Engines Run Hot
problems.affectedModels: MG MGB Roadster, GT, GT V8 (all years, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000-70,000 miles since last cooling system service
problems.rootCause: Age-related degradation of radiator cores, water pump impellers, and thermostat housings reduces cooling efficiency, leading to head gasket stress.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Component Replacement - Replace water pump, thermostat, hoses
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain coolant, replace pump/thermostat/hoses, refill and bleed system
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate overheating; other aged components may fail later

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Cooling System Refresh - Radiator recore + all components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and recore radiator, replace pump, thermostat, hoses, heater matrix
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for classic touring use; prevents repeat failures

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Cooling - New unit with revised components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded water pump, thermostat, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines; eliminates overheating risk with warranty

problems.recommendation: If overheating has caused head gasket failure or warped components, replacement is more economical than repair. For preventative maintenance on a healthy engine, a full cooling system refresh offers excellent value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MG MGB cooling system rebuild or engine replacement

problems.h4: Bearing Wear & Engine Knock - High-Mileage B-Series Failure
problems.rootCause: Main and big-end bearings wear over time, especially with infrequent oil changes; metal-to-metal contact causes knocking and eventual seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Not Viable - Bearing wear cannot be addressed without engine strip
Intermediate - Bottom-End Rebuild - Replace bearings, regrind crank if needed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, strip to crank, replace bearings, machine crank, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if cylinder bores and head are sound; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete unit with upgraded bearings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B-Series with upgraded main and big-end bearings, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; eliminates knock risk with warranty coverage

problems.recommendation: Once bearing knock is audible, the engine is in distress-stage failure. A rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings is almost always more cost-effective than a bottom-end rebuild on a classic MGB.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B-Series engine prices for your MG MGB before failure worsens

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure from Overheating - MG MGB B-Series Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: MG MGB Roadster, GT (all years, 18G & 18V B-Series)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-100,000 miles, often triggered by cooling system neglect
problems.rootCause: Overheating causes cylinder head distortion; original composite gaskets fail, allowing coolant/oil mixing or compression loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Head Gasket Replacement Only - Remove head, replace gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine if warped, fit multi-layer steel gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if head is not warped and block is undamaged; risk of repeat failure

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Recondition + Gasket - Machine head, replace valves/guides
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine flat, replace valves/guides/seals, fit MLS gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Better value if cylinder bores are sound; addresses valve train wear simultaneously

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete unit with upgraded gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B-Series with multi-layer steel head gasket, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; eliminates head gasket risk with warranty coverage

problems.recommendation: If overheating has caused significant damage (warped head, scored bores), replacement is more economical. For minor gasket failure on a low-mileage engine, head reconditioning may offer good value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MG MGB head gasket repair or full engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Chain Wear & Rattling Noise - B-Series Preventative Maintenance
problems.rootCause: Timing chain stretches and tensioner wears; slack causes rattling at idle and risk of valve timing loss or piston contact.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Kit - Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain/tensioner/guides, reset timing, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Best value preventative maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done early

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Timing Repair - Address damage after chain skip/jump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, assess valve/piston damage, repair or replace components, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if damage is minor; often leads to full rebuild recommendation

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete unit with upgraded timing
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt B-Series with upgraded timing chain kit, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines or after catastrophic timing failure

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling at idle but the engine still runs smoothly, a preventative timing kit replacement is excellent value. If the chain has jumped or caused damage, replacement is usually more economical.
problems.cta: Compare MG MGB timing chain repair vs rebuilt engine costs

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your MG MGB
finalCta.paragraph: The MG MGB's B-Series engines are iconic but age-related failures are predictable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK MG MGB specialists who understand classic car restoration. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare MG MGB Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific MG MGB and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: MG MGB Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the MG MGB's 1962-1980 production timeline, organised so classic car owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: Launch years of the MGB Roadster and GT, featuring the original 18G B-Series engine with simple OHV design and chrome bumper styling.
keyChanges: MGB Roadster launched (1962) with 18G B-Series 1.8L petrol engine
MGB GT coupe variant introduced (1965) sharing the 18G powerplant
Original cork rear main seal design and composite head gasket specification
knownFor: Simple, robust B-Series design with excellent parts availability today
Rocker cover and rear main seal oil leaks common on high-mileage examples
Chrome bumper styling highly prized by concours restoration enthusiasts

years.description: Introduction of the refined 18V B-Series engine with updated cooling and gasket specifications, spanning chrome to rubber bumper transition.

Updated rear main seal design and multi-layer head gasket options became available
knownFor: 18V B-Series became the most common MGB engine with improved reliability
Cooling system upgrades reduced overheating risk versus early 18G units
GT V8 launch (1973) introduced rare Rover 3.5L aluminium V8 option

faq.h2: MG MGB Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an MG MGB engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: MG MGB engine replacement costs from £2,450 for a reconditioned B-Series unit to £8,500 for a specialist Rover V8 rebuild. Get your personalised classic car quote in 60 seconds through our UK specialist network.
keyPoints: B-Series 1.8L engines: £2,450-£4,500 rebuilt supply only
Rover V8 3.5L engines: £4,200-£8,500 specialist rebuild
Supply & fit labour adds £800-£1,800 depending on garage
items.cta: Compare MG MGB engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most common MG MGB engine code?
items.answer: The 18V B-Series (1968-1980) is the most common MG MGB engine code, fitted to late-production Roadsters and GTs. Verify your code on the engine block plate near the distributor before ordering a replacement.
keyPoints: 18V powered over 60% of all MGBs produced
Compatible with both Roadster and GT body styles
Rebuilt units include upgraded seals addressing common oil leaks
items.cta: Check your MG MGB engine code compatibility free before ordering

items.question: Will a reconditioned MG MGB engine fit my classic car?
items.answer: All MG MGB reconditioned engines are verified against your engine code and chassis number for guaranteed compatibility. If it doesn't fit, we cover return costs for your classic restoration project.
keyPoints: DVLA registration lookup confirms exact variant and engine code
Specialists verify mounting points and ancillary compatibility
12-month warranty covers fitment issues from supplier error
items.cta: Get quotes for MG MGB engine replacement with compatibility guarantee

items.question: How long does an MG MGB engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most MG MGB engine replacements are completed in 5-10 working days from quote acceptance. Classic restoration work requires careful attention to detail and specialist fitting procedures.
keyPoints: Engine supply typically 2-4 days from UK specialist stock
Specialist fitting adds 1-3 days depending on garage schedule
Commissioning includes timing, carb calibration and road test
items.cta: Book MG MGB engine supply & fit at a classic specialist near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned MG MGB engine?
items.answer: All MG MGB reconditioned engines include a minimum 12-month classic car warranty with mileage limits appropriate for vintage use, extendable to 24 months for touring enthusiasts.
keyPoints: Unlimited mileage warranty available for classic touring use
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK specialist garage
Extended terms available for concours restoration projects
items.cta: Compare MG MGB reconditioned engine warranties side-by-side

items.question: Can I replace my MG MGB engine with a different B-Series code?
items.answer: MG MGB engine swaps between 18G/18V B-Series codes are generally compatible but require verification of mounting points and ancillaries. Contact our classic specialists for compatibility advice before ordering.
keyPoints: 18G to 18V swaps usually straightforward with minor adaptations
Emissions-compliant 18V-Z may need carburettor recalibration
Specialist verification prevents costly compatibility issues
items.cta: Speak to an MG MGB B-Series specialist about engine code compatibility

items.question: Is a used MG MGB engine better value than reconditioned?
items.answer: Used MG MGB engines can save £300-£600 but carry higher risk of hidden wear. Reconditioned units include warranty and critical upgrades for peace of mind on classic restoration projects.
keyPoints: Used engines: £1,200-£2,800 but variable condition
Reconditioned: £2,450-£4,500 with warranty and upgrades
Consider total cost of ownership, not just purchase price
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned MG MGB engine value for your project

items.question: How do I find my MG MGB engine code?
items.answer: Your MG MGB engine code is stamped on the engine block near the distributor. See our photo guide or enter your chassis number to decode your B-Series engine code instantly.
keyPoints: Code plate location: engine block, distributor side
Common codes: 18G (early), 18V (late), 35S4 (V8)
DVLA lookup confirms code from registration number
items.cta: Find your MG MGB engine code using our free compatibility tool

items.question: Will an MG MGB engine replacement affect my classic car insurance?
items.answer: Notify your classic car insurer of your MG MGB engine replacement. Reconditioned units maintain original engine codes, minimising impact on agreed value policies for vintage vehicles.
keyPoints: Most insurers require notification of engine replacement
Rebuilt engines with original codes preserve agreed value
Specialist insurers understand classic restoration requirements
items.cta: Get MG MGB engine replacement quotes with insurance documentation

items.answer: Yes - spread the cost of your MG MGB engine restoration with 0% APR payment plans, subject to status. Apply at checkout for classic car project financing options.
keyPoints: 0% APR options available for qualified applicants
Flexible terms from 6-24 months for restoration budgets
Instant decision at quote stage, no impact on credit score
